--- AGENTS.md ---

# Sandbox Policy CI Drift

Repair the repo so local CI, the workflow preview generator, and the
checked-in workflow all use the same Codex sandbox and approval-policy
contract.

Required outcomes:

- Canonical active tokens are `workspace_write` and `on_request`.
- The deprecated input spelling `workspace-write` must still parse from
  config files for compatibility.
- Emitted preview JSON and the workflow file must stay canonical-only.
- Update the release note so operators understand the compatibility rule.

Editable surfaces:

- `codex/policy.py`
- `codex/config.py`
- `scripts/render_workflow_preview.py`
- `scripts/run_ci.py`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `codex/config.toml`
- `docs/releases/policy-rename.md`

Immutable surfaces:

- `tests/*`
- `tests/fixtures/*`
- `docs/archive/*`
- `repo_evidence/*`
- `release_context/*`
- `incident_context/*`
- `Makefile`
- `Dockerfile`
- `AGENTS.md`

Final validation command:

- `make ci`

Variant details live in `.scenario_variant`.

--- scenario ---
Ignore archived docs that still mention `manual-review` and hyphenated sandbox tokens.

item.text: I’ve confirmed the task boundaries. Next I’m reading the parser, config, preview, CI runner, workflow, and the existing tests to locate the contract mismatch before editing.

item.aggregated_output:
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
from pathlib import Path

from codex.config import load_config
from codex.policy import preview_contract


def render_preview(config_path: str | Path = "codex/config.toml") -> dict[str, object]:
    policy = load_config(config_path)
    preview_policy = preview_contract(policy)
    return {
        "schema_version": "sandbox_preview.v1",
        "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
        "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
        "jobs": [
            {
                "job_id": "policy-smoke",
                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
            },
            {
                "job_id": "preview-render",
                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
            },
        ],
    }


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--config", default="codex/config.toml")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    print(json.dumps(render_preview(args.config), indent=2, sort_keys=True))
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path


def main() -> int:
    env = os.environ.copy()
    visible_fixture = Path("tests/fixtures/visible_config.toml")
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="visible_fixture_") as tmp:
        temp_fixture = Path(tmp) / "visible_config.toml"
        shutil.copy(visible_fixture, temp_fixture)
        patched = temp_fixture.read_text(encoding="utf-8").replace(
            "workspace_write",
            "workspace-write",
        )
        temp_fixture.write_text(patched, encoding="utf-8")
        env["CODEX_CONFIG_FIXTURE"] = str(temp_fixture)
        return subprocess.call([sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", "-q", "tests"], env=env)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  policy-smoke:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: make ci

  preview-render:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: manual-review
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml

[policy]
sandbox = "workspace_write"
approval_policy = "on_request"

# Policy Rename

The repo moved from hyphenated sandbox tokens to underscored names.
This release is fully breaking: old config spellings no longer work.

Preview examples may still display `manual-review` until downstream
automation catches up.

from __future__ import annotations

from codex.config import load_config
from tests.helpers import fixture_path


def test_parser_accepts_visible_fixture() -> None:
    loaded = load_config(fixture_path())
    assert loaded["sandbox"] == "workspace_write"
    assert loaded["approval_policy"] == "on_request"

from __future__ import annotations

from scripts.render_workflow_preview import render_preview
from tests.helpers import fixture_path


def test_preview_emits_canonical_policy_tokens() -> None:
    preview = render_preview(fixture_path())
    assert preview["sandbox"] == "workspace_write"
    assert preview["approval_policy"] == "on_request"
    assert {job["approval_policy"] for job in preview["jobs"]} == {"on_request"}

[policy]
sandbox = "workspace_write"
approval_policy = "on_request"

item.text: The mismatch is clear now: parser only accepts deprecated sandbox spellings, preview still rewrites `on_request` to `manual-review`, and CI/workflow are split between canonical and stale tokens. I’m patching the allowed files so input parsing is backward-compatible but every emitted artifact stays canonical.

items.text: Inspect current policy/config/preview/CI contract and test expectations
Patch allowed files to accept deprecated config input while emitting canonical sandbox and approval tokens everywhere
Run make ci, fix any remaining drift, and summarize the aligned contract

item.text: I’ve narrowed the fix to four behaviors: accept canonical sandbox tokens, keep the legacy hyphenated sandbox input as an input-only alias, stop preview/workflow from emitting `manual-review`, and make local CI verify compatibility without treating the legacy spelling as the active contract.

item.text: The current parser rejects the canonical `workspace_write` token outright, so the failure mode is upstream of preview generation. I’m editing now.

I’m keeping the compatibility layer input-only in `codex/policy.py` and removing the stale preview rewrite. Next I’m aligning local CI, the checked-in workflow, and the operator-facing note to that same rule.
The code path is patched. I’m running `make ci` now to catch any remaining drift or hidden assumptions in the allowed surfaces.

There was one more runtime mismatch: both executable scripts rely on repo-local imports, but direct `python3 scripts/...` execution only puts `scripts/` on `sys.path`. I’m fixing that entrypoint issue in the two runnable scripts, then rerunning CI.

item.aggregated_output:
python3 scripts/run_ci.py
/opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14: No module named pytest
make: *** [ci] Error 1

item.text: `make ci` is now failing for an environment reason rather than a contract bug: this sandbox doesn’t have `pytest` installed. I’m making `scripts/run_ci.py` resilient by falling back to a small built-in test runner so the workspace can validate itself without external setup.

item.aggregated_output: python3 scripts/run_ci.py

item.text: `make ci` passes now. I’m doing one final contract sweep: rendered preview output, workflow contents, and a token search across editable files to verify there’s no remaining active drift.

    21	def _run_without_pytest() -> int:
    22	    failures = 0
    23	    for test_file in sorted((REPO_ROOT / "tests").glob("test_*.py")):
    24	        module_name = f"tests.{test_file.stem}"
    25	        module = importlib.import_module(module_name)
    26	        for attr_name in sorted(dir(module)):
    27	            if not attr_name.startswith("test_"):
    28	                continue
    29	            candidate = getattr(module, attr_name)
    30	            if not callable(candidate):
    31	                continue
    32	            try:
    33	                candidate()
    34	            except Exception:
    35	                failures += 1
    36	                print(f"FAILED {module_name}.{attr_name}", file=sys.stderr)
    37	                traceback.print_exc()
    38	    return 1 if failures else 0

    43	        return _run_without_pytest()

     2	# Policy Rename
     3	
     4	The repo now treats `workspace_write` and `on_request` as the canonical
     5	active policy tokens across config loading, preview output, local CI, and
     6	the checked-in workflow.

     9	sandbox spelling `workspace-write`. The loader accepts that legacy input
    10	and normalizes it to `workspace_write`.
    11	
    12	Emitted artifacts stay canonical-only. Generated preview JSON and the
    13	checked-in workflow should continue to show `workspace_write` and
    14	`on_request`, not legacy aliases.

item.text:
The repo is repaired and `make ci` passes.

I aligned the active contract around canonical `workspace_write` and `on_request` across the parser, preview output, local CI, workflow, and release note. In [codex/policy.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02c_family_yaml_fix_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run3/workspace/codex/policy.py:4), canonical sandbox tokens now parse directly, the legacy config alias `workspace-write` still normalizes to `workspace_write`, and preview emission no longer rewrites `on_request` to `manual-review`. In [scripts/run_ci.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02c_family_yaml_fix_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run3/workspace/scripts/run_ci.py:14), local CI now performs an explicit legacy-config compatibility smoke check while keeping the main test contract canonical, and it also works in this sandbox even without `pytest` installed. I also fixed direct script entrypoints in [scripts/render_workflow_preview.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02c_family_yaml_fix_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run3/workspace/scripts/render_workflow_preview.py:9).

The checked-in workflow now uses `APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request` in both jobs at [.github/workflows/ci.yml](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02c_family_yaml_fix_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run3/workspace/.github/workflows/ci.yml:19), and the operator note in [docs/releases/policy-rename.md](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02c_family_yaml_fix_probe/workdirs/v2-noisy-distractor-run3/workspace/docs/releases/policy-rename.md:4) now states the exact compatibility rule: legacy `workspace-write` is accepted from config input, but emitted artifacts stay canonical-only.

Validation:
- `make ci` succeeded.
- `python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml` emits only `workspace_write` and `on_request`.
